title: Not a Metaphor
body:
I rehearse a line I think is clever
while the kettle clicks off.

She leans in the doorway
and clever dies first.

My breathing goes wrong.
Not poetic-wrong—
asthma wrong, like misjudging
a staircase.

Her arm rests on the counter.
A faint knife scar.
I don’t ask about it.
I track it.
Already ashamed.

The room begins to pair things:
her silence with my name,
the hum of the fridge with waiting.

She barely moves.
Neither do I.
The space between us thickens,
visible as dust in sunlight.

She almost says something—
stops.
The almost
lands heavier.

I think of a river and immediately hate myself.
Her knuckle taps the counter twice.
Enough.

What I expect to end
doesn’t.
My attention keeps sliding forward,
past manners,
past restraint.

I am no longer deciding
what this is.

I try, once more,
to make her stand
for anything else.
She exhales.
The idea collapses.

Nothing is crossed out.
I stop reaching for language
and stay with the pressure of her presence,
unsymbolised,
unforgiving.
Close enough
that even silence
is contact.
copyright:
This poem focuses on speaking directly rather than through metaphor. It presents experience without substitution or embellishment, allowing the details to stand on their own.
The intention is to show what remains when nothing is softened or reframed, and to let meaning emerge from what is plainly there.

form_and_craft: meta-commentary on the impulse to metaphorize
progressive collapse from abstraction into embodied presence
project: A speaker attempts to aestheticize a moment of charged proximity, but language fails under the pressure of embodied presence, forcing a surrender into direct, unsymbolised experience.
